explanations.1: This line tells the system to use the bats test runner program to execute this file, similar to how #!/bin/bash works for shell scripts.
explanations.4: This loads a helper library called bats-support that provides useful utilities for writing tests.
explanations.5: This loads bats-assert, another helper library that gives you assertion functions like assert_output and assert_success.
explanations.9: The 'run' keyword executes the echo command and captures its output and exit status so you can check them in the next lines.
explanations.16: The --regexp flag tells assert_output to check if the output matches a regular expression pattern instead of an exact string match; [Ss] means it accepts either capital or lowercase S.
